name: fhir
description: A Rust implementation of the HL7 FHIR R5 data model, plus a spec-driven code generator that produces it from the official FHIR specification JSON. Every resource and datatype is a serde-serializable Rust type that round-trips to and from canonical FHIR JSON.

modules.r5::resources: The 158 R5 resources as structs, plus the polymorphic `Resource` enum tagged by `resourceType`.
modules.r5::types: The ~50 complex datatypes and 21 primitive newtypes. Primitives serialize transparently to their underlying JSON value.
modules.r5::meta: Per-element metadata extracted from the specification (cardinality, bindings, value[x] choice types, reference target profiles, summary membership), queryable by FHIR path.
modules.r5::choice: Support types for value[x] choice enums (Primitive<T>) plus the FhirChoice derive.
modules.r5::coded: The Coded<E> wrapper typing required-binding fields as their codes enum with an Unknown(String) fallback.
modules.r5::extension_ext: The ExtensionExt/ModifierExtensionExt traits: extension(url), extensions(url), set_extension, add_extension.
modules.r5::builder: BuilderError for the generated Type::builder() builders.
modules.r5::bundle_util: Bundle iteration (iter_resources, resources::<T>), next_link paging, and the transaction/batch builder.
modules.prelude: Crate-root prelude (use fhir::prelude::*) re-exporting the most-used resources, datatypes, Validate, Coded, and ExtensionExt.
modules.r5::temporal: Parsing and precision-aware comparison for the date/time primitives (Date::parse_parts, DateParts, FHIR precision ordering).
modules.r5::parse: The spec-driven code generator that reads the FHIR specification JSON in `DEFINITIONS_DIR`.
modules.r5::abstract_types: Base/abstract FHIR interfaces used by the generator.

cardinality_mapping.value[x]: one field per allowed type, named value_<type>

examples.build_patient: Construct a Patient and serialize to canonical FHIR JSON.
examples.validate_resource: Validate a resource and inspect ValidationIssues.
examples.read_bundle: Read a Bundle and dispatch on each entry's resource type.
examples.code_systems: Use CodeSystem enums as type-safe coded values.

documentation.CONTRIBUTING.md: Build/test commands and the code-generator workflow.
documentation.AGENTS.md: Guidance for AI coding agents and contributors.
documentation.spec/: The living specifications (the source of truth).
